rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in annex agree blocks; use component type annexes for assumptions/guarantees and implementation annexes for bindings.
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE code are visible in the target AADL scope.
generic_rules: Use assign statements only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation scope.
Write type scope guarantees relating visible outputs to visible inputs, avoiding implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
Avoid encoding AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time or event ports as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings should appear after relevant subcomponents and connections in implementation scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type scope for such contracts.
Do not reference AADL properties or event ports as numeric or Boolean values in AGREE expressions.
